rag_rules: Repair validator diagnostics by adding missing annex syntax elements without changing unrelated architecture
Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantees
Place guarantees in the component type annex when they relate to visible outputs and inputs
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations and comparisons
generic_rules: Repair validator diagnostics without changing unrelated architecture
Use only visible identifiers for guarantees and assumptions
Place guarantees in the component type annex when they relate to visible outputs and inputs
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations and comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `guarantee "label": expression;` for output range guarantees
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for helper declarations
Use `const name : type = value;` for constant declarations
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not add architecture to fix a guarantee expression with type <error>
Do not use AGREE type names like integer or boolean; use int, real, and bool instead
